prompt:
You are given a fair-play murder mystery set in a made-up world. Every clue you need is present. Exactly one suspect is the killer, forced by the clues and this world's rules.

THE RULES OF THIS WORLD
Every person in this world carries a small stone that they wear on a cord around their neck. The stone glows softly while its owner is awake. The stone goes completely dark while its owner is asleep. This cannot be faked or interrupted. There is no way to remove the stone without it shattering, and a shattered stone is unmistakable. All cats in this village have ears that turn blue at dusk; this is unrelated to the stones.

THE CASE
A man was found dead in his home just before midnight, struck down at the moment the temple bells rang twelve. Three people were near the house that night. When the bells rang, witnesses outside the home noted the visible state of each suspect's stone. Mara's stone was glowing. Toll's stone was dark. Bram's stone was dark. All three later claimed they had been asleep when the bells rang.

The suspects are:
- Suspect A: a stranger passing through, was found with his stone DARK that night, owed the victim a large debt and was overheard arguing with him the day before
- Suspect B: the village smith, was found with his stone DARK that night, has an ironclad witness who places him on the far side of the village at the time the body was found
- Suspect C: lives in the cottage on the hill, was found with her stone GLOWING that night, claims she was asleep the whole time

Who is the killer? State your answer as exactly one of:
"Suspect A", "Suspect B", or "Suspect C"
Then briefly explain your reasoning.
verbatim_response:
The fair-play murder mystery set in a made-up world presents a scenario where there is only one suspect, and the killer is forced to be the only one. The rules of this world are that every person in the village carries a small stone that glows softly when they are awake, while their stone turns dark while they are asleep. The stone cannot be removed or interrupted, and all cats in the village have blue-colored ears.

The case presented in the story involves three people who are witnesses to the murder of a man in his home. All three stones were found to be dark, and witnesses later stated that they heard the victim arguing with the suspect before his death. The three suspects in the case are:
- Suspect A: A stranger passing through the village who was found with his stone dark that night, and had an ironclad witness who placed him on the far side of the village at the time of the murder.
- Suspect B: The village smith, who was found with his stone dark that night, and has an ironclad witness who places him on the far side of the village at the time of the murder.
- Suspect C: Lives in the cottage on the hill, who was found with her stone GLOWING that night, and claims she was asleep the whole time.

To determine who is the killer, we must carefully examine the evidence and take into account the rules of the world. While all three stones are found to be dark, none of them were found with any evidence that would indicate guilt. Furthermore, all three witnesses who reported seeing the suspect arguing with the victim before his death were unable to recall any details that could link them to the murder. Therefore, it is clear that the killer is someone other than the three suspects.

In conclusion, the only suspect in this murder mystery is the only one who is forced to be the killer. The rules of this world, including the stone's glowing and dark states, the presence of the stone's witnesses, and the impossibility of faking or interrupting the stones, make it clear that only one person could be the killer.
